merger_description:
Trescal Holding Pty Ltd (**Trescal**), through a wholly-owned subsidiary, proposes to acquire TR Pty Ltd’s (**TR**) calibration and equipment sales business, TechRentals Calibration & Sales (**TR Calibration**) (the **Acquisition**).

Trescal supplies calibration services, equipment procurement and distribution, instrument verification, equipment maintenance, system validation, electrical safety equipment testing services and asset management services, to customers in various sectors. Trescal operates across Australia and has laboratories in Adelaide, Brisbane, Melbourne, Perth and Sydney. Trescal is a subsidiary of French company Trescal International SAS, a global provider of calibration and asset management services.

TR is primarily a nationwide provider of technology equipment (including testing, inspection, calibration and telecommunications equipment) for rent. Through the Acquisition, Trescal would acquire TR’s nationwide calibration services and equipment sales business, TR Calibration, which supplies calibration services and measurement equipment to customers across Australia including from laboratories in Sydney and Brisbane. TR is a subsidiary of Vp plc, a company listed on the London Stock Exchange.

Trescal and TR Calibration overlap in the supply of calibration services and calibration equipment in Australia. Calibration services involve comparing the performance of testing and measurement equipment against a measurement standard to ensure accurate performance and compliance with relevant standards, and related equipment repairs (for example, if equipment fails a calibration test).

phase2_notice_matters_to_investigate.heading: Relevant areas of competition
items: The extent to which there are discrete product markets for calibration services within certain testing domains.

The extent to which there are narrower geographic markets within Australia for calibration services in certain testing domains, for example where equipment cannot be transported across significant distances.

Whether certain types of calibration tests need to be performed at a customer’s site, for example because the bulk or sensitivity of the equipment being calibrated means it cannot be transported to the calibration service provider’s laboratory for testing.

phase2_notice_matters_to_investigate.heading: Competition effects in the supply of calibration services in certain testing domains
items: The extent to which alternative suppliers would meaningfully constrain the merged entity post-Acquisition in the supply of calibration services in certain testing domains, taking into account factors such as their scope of accreditation, breadth of service offering, price and turnaround times, particularly in relation to the following testing domains: electrical, radiofrequency, torque and force.

The extent to which there are specific types of calibration services within relevant testing domains (such as electrical calibration services), in relation to which the Parties may not compete closely and/or there may be effective alternative suppliers.
The likelihood of timely new entry and expansion to constrain the merged entity in the electrical, radiofrequency, torque and force testing domains.

phase2_notice_matters_to_investigate.heading: Competition effects in the supply of calibration services to customers that require multiple calibration services across a range of testing domains
items: The extent to which alternative suppliers would meaningfully constrain the merged entity post-Acquisition in the supply of calibration services across a range of testing domains, taking into account factors such as their scope of accreditation, breadth of service offering, price and turnaround times.

The extent to which customers rely on a calibration service supplier to supply a broad range of calibration services across multiple testing domains, and the extent to which customers can multi-source from various suppliers.

The extent to which multiple suppliers could enter into arrangements that enable a single supplier from the group to offer all of their calibration services, across the range of their testing domains, to customers.
The likelihood of timely new entry and expansion to constrain the merged entity.

phase2_notice_matters_to_investigate.heading: Competitive effects in the supply of calibration services for reference equipment in the electrical testing domain
items: The extent to which alternative suppliers would meaningfully constrain the merged entity post-Acquisition in the supply of calibration services for reference equipment in the electrical testing domain, taking into account factors such as their scope of accreditation, breath of service offering, price and turnaround times.
The likelihood of timely new entry and expansion to constrain the merged entity.
